title: ocimum waterbergense
intro: Ocimum waterbergense, also known as the Waterberg Basil, is a species of plant in the family Lamiaceae, closely related to the basil plant. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Ocimum waterbergense include its ability to reduce fever, relieve digestive issues, and soothe skin irritations. Ocimum waterbergense can be prepared as a herbal tea, infused oil, or dried extract, and is often used in traditional medicine to promote overall well-being.
